column24: Takeaways: Can't say yet. Habituation to the water jet could possibly occur in some cases, but the inter-lane variability is too extreme. Some worms are getting blasted off the ground, which could dishabituate anyways. Worms also crawl up mesh. The device will need upgrades before a conclusion can be confidently drawn.

column23: Testing for habituation to UV with same parameters as it would have in CC. I just ran CC without anything attached to the actuator, so in effect was just US only. Planarians really love going up tubes. Used 1 UV lamp.

Note that water is recycled, which was probably not a good idea, since UV probably releases stress signaling substances, at definitely gunky slime. This can be fixed in a future re-run with a better trough. Used 1 UV lamp.
column24: Takeaways: Yay! The block averages are 91% and 92.6% for contractions to UV within the first 30 frames, when using one of the UV lamps. This is pretty good. And, preferable to shock, even qui worms will show a small but visible head contraction and sometimes hightail it out of quiescence. However, many worms disappeared, likely up the tubes, so these results need to be taken with some salt.

These worms have very strong adhesion to the dishes, which is in accordance with the GT's we got from Levin lab. They also seem to gun for the corners with gumption, so they may need to be encouraged back to the center frequently (i.e. babysat during the blocks). Worm 3 does have a lesion on tail, near pharynx. 3/5 worms had lesions/gouges after trials, probably as consequence of high rate of experiment encouragement. Use widened tip next time.

One worm recovered after yesterday's final block who was hiding under trough, so average p(CR) should lag until asymptote, i.e. let's be a little bit more patient with these worms. 25 tri, 2 min ITI.
